title: Raphionacme hirsuta
taxonomy: Raphionacme hirsuta is classified within the plant family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The genus Raphionacme is a small group of plants endemic to southern Africa, with Raphionacme hirsuta being one of its species. Raphionacme hirsuta is further classified as a succulent shrub, with its hirsute stems and leaves providing adaptations for water storage and drought tolerance. The plant's classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its flower structure and leaf shape, as well as its genetic relationships to other plants within the Apocynaceae family. Overall, the classification of Raphionacme hirsuta provides valuable insights into its evolutionary history and relationships to other plants.

intro: Raphionacme hirsuta, also known as the hairy raphionacme, is a succulent plant species native to southern Africa. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Raphionacme hirsuta include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
